mnemonics: USE ASTERISK FOR A WHOLE LIBRARY, SPECIFY CLASS FOR A SINGLE BOOK
keyPoints: Import statements allow you to use classes from other packages without the fully qualified name
You can import a single class (e.g., `import java.util.ArrayList;`) or all classes from a package using a wildcard (e.g., `import java.util.*;`)
The `java.lang` package is automatically imported, so you don't need to import classes like String or System
commonMistakes: Using wildcard imports unnecessarily, which can lead to naming conflicts and reduced code clarity
Forgetting that import statements only bring in classes, not sub-packages (importing `java.util.*` doesn't import `java.util.stream.*`)
Trying to import classes from the default package (classes without a package declaration) which is not allowed
